spec_match.rationale: Audits 12 at-rules with CSS.supports('at-rule(@x)') and gates a five-level progressive component on @layer, @container, @property, @scope, and @starting-style.

interactive.rationale: The support table renders live, level pips update from real probes, and per-level force-off checkboxes let users inspect each fallback state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'live audit of 12 at-rules … a card component that progressively enhances through 5 levels … green pips show which levels are active in your browser' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers unsupported at-rule() detection and now exposes forced-off fallback states for each enhancement level.

references.rationale: See-also now links directly to chromestatus.com/feature/5110744177836032 and the CSS Conditional 5 spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: Raw hex colors were replaced with design-system variables, status styling moved to classes, and pips use token-backed states.

summary: The upgrade-path concept now follows the design system, includes the required ChromeStatus/spec references, and lets users force individual enhancement levels off to inspect fallback behavior.
